title: Information Through Noise: Uncertainty, Capacity, and Reliable Codes
description: This is a demonstration course that shows what Coursewerk can produce; it is not a course package intended for direct use in real teaching.  This two-chapter introductory information-science demo begins by turning uncertainty into a measurable quantity: bits, entropy, conditional entropy, mutual information, noisy channels, and capacity. It then asks how real messages survive corruption, using redundancy, Hamming distance, parity checks, block codes, interleaving, and layered validation to explain error detection and correction without implying perfect reliability. Each chapter includes a concept map, accessible study guide, visual slide deck, assessment-design guide, and worked practice set. The package is generated automatically by an AI agent following Coursewerk from revision-pinned Wikipedia pages and retained rights evidence, without human-authored lesson content or subject-matter editing; human involvement is limited to commissioning, pipeline audit, visual review, and publication oversight. AI generation resulted residual flaws are reserved intentionally.

chapters.title: Measuring Information: Surprise, Entropy, and Channel Capacity
Reliable Codes: Redundancy, Distance, and Error Correction
